---
title: "resolv.conf gets over-written"
date: 2009-03-02
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by asundaresan on 2009-03-02
Hello:

I'm facing a peculiar problem on my laptop. Whenever I use dhcp with my eth0 (ethernet cable) I get the proper dns address from dhclient and it is written to **/etc/resolv.conf**.

```

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 192.168.2.1

```

After a while it gets overwritten, I don't know how and it now looks like this. (Note missing nameserver line)
```

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN

```
I have not done anything with the machine in the meantime. If I copy the original file the network works again. I'd be glad to hear any suggestions.

Cheers,
Aravind.

---

### Post by Mud.Knee.Havoc on 2009-03-02
I had to completely uninstall the network manager for it to stop "helping me" by resetting this all the time.  

I've searched for a better way, but if I'm not mistaken, the developers somehow see this as a feature. :confused:
